subject.title: Business Management
subject.description:
**IB Business Management** is a **Group 3** subject in the **International Baccalaureate Diploma Programme (IBDP)**. It explores **business fundamentals, human resource management, finance and accounts, marketing, and operations management**, with a strong focus on **case study analysis, real-world business applications, and strategic decision-making**.

The course is available at **Standard Level (SL, 150 teaching hours)** and **Higher Level (HL, 240 teaching hours)**, with **first assessment in 2024**.

### Assessment at a Glance

| **Assessment** | **Format** | **SL** | **HL** | **Weighting** |
|----------------|------------|--------|--------|---------------|
| **Paper 1** | Case study-based exam | 1h 30min | 1h 30min | SL: 35%, HL: 25% |
| **Paper 2** | Core themes exam | 1h 30min | 1h 30min | SL: 35%, HL: 30% |
| **Paper 3** | Social enterprise case study | N/A | 1h 15min | HL: 25% |
| **Internal Assessment** | Written commentary (SL) / Research project (HL) | Various | Various | SL: 30%, HL: 20% |

strengths: Consistent use of the key concept âchangeâ throughout introduction, analysis and conclusion
Clear analytical links between Nvidiaâs strategic shift and the concept of change
Strong context-setting of the H100 launch in relation to organizational strategy
weaknesses: Integration remains descriptive at times rather than deeply analytical
Key concept not fully threaded through every minor analytical point
Opportunities exist to deepen the causal analysis of how change drives competitive advantage
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: Strand assessments place this work firmly in band 4: the concept of change is explicitly adopted, linked to Nvidiaâs shift across multiple sections, and supported with data. The occasional descriptive treatment and lack of fully sustained analytical depth preclude the top band (5), so a score of 4 accurately reflects strong but partially integrated understanding.

strengths: Appropriate number of supporting documents (four) fulfilling IB requirements
Relevant sources (investor reports, media interviews) directly tied to market-cap and revenue data
weaknesses: Sources rely heavily on headline figures with limited internal or contrasting perspectives
Depth of insight is adequate but not wide-ranging; lacks diversity of viewpoints
Inâtext citations could be more transparent and consistently formatted
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: While the student supplies the correct quantity of documents and clear relevance (band 3 by count), the depth is modestâmost insights stem from topâlevel figures without deeper proprietary or analytical sources. This aligns more closely with band 2 (âgenerally relevant but lack depthâ), thus meriting a score of 2.

strengths: Appropriate selection of the BCG matrix and P/E ratio as analytical tools
Definitions and context for tools are well explained
Tools are applied to Nvidiaâs data rather than generic examples
weaknesses: Analytical relevance sometimes slips into narrative description without explicit strategic linkage
Toolkit is adequate but not especially broad or inventive for an HL investigation
Occasional gaps in tying tool outputs back to the research question
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: The work consistently applies two relevant tools to Nvidiaâs data, matching band 3 descriptors for âadequate selectionâ and âadequate application.â It falls short of band 4âs requirement for clear and fully effective application, so a holistic score of 3 is appropriate.
feedback.groupName: Selection and Application of Tools and Theories

strengths: Uses multiple current data sets (market-cap trends, P/E ratios, revenue breakdown) with clear graphical presentation
Comparative discussion (e.g., versus Intel) adds evaluative dimension
Pie chart and revenue figures effectively illustrate portfolio shifts
weaknesses: Analysis is often descriptive with limited interrogation of alternative explanations or external factors
Few reflections on data limitations or deeper strategic implications
Some suggestions (e.g., integrating percentages) remain unimplemented, weakening analytical rigor
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: Data selection is sufficient (band 4), but the evaluative component remains at âsome analysisâ (band 3). The student comments on trends but rarely probes deeper causes or limitations. Thus the combined Analysis & Evaluation criterion warrants a score of 3.

strengths: Conclusions are fully consistent with the presented evidence (revenue shares, market-cap growth)
Makes an explicit, clear statement answering the research question
Draws together quantitative findings into a coherent closing argument
weaknesses: Limited critical reflection on possible alternative factors beyond the H100
Could strengthen by acknowledging study limitations
Concluding discussion could integrate more of the key concept analytically
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: The conclusion clearly and directly addresses the research question and aligns with the evidence, satisfying band 3 descriptors for consistency and completeness. No contradictory data are introduced, so the full mark of 3 is justified.

strengths: Logical organization with title page, contents, introduction, analysis sections and conclusion
Clear headings that guide the reader through the argument
Consistent flow and academic structure throughout
weaknesses: Minor numbering and TOC alignment issues (missing section â3.â)
Some repetitive phrasing in the final paragraph suggests tighter editing could improve conciseness
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: The report demonstrates an appropriate and coherent structure throughout, fulfilling band 2 criteria. Small organisational hiccups do not undermine overall navigability, so the full score of 2 is awarded.

strengths: All IB-required presentation elements are included (RQ, word count, contents page, references, figures)
Table of contents and labeling enhance navigation
Consistent formatting of core elements
weaknesses: Inconsistent list indentation and mixed citation styles
Several spelling/typo errors (e.g., âNyidiaâ, âEarninasâ) and figure-label mismatches
Figure axes/labels are sometimes missing, reducing interpretability
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: The student includes every required element, satisfying band 2. Presentation shortcomings (formatting inconsistencies, typos, figure labelling issues) are noticeable but do not omit mandatory components, justifying the full 2 points.

strandSelections.reasoning: The report follows a logical structure (title page, contents, introduction, analysis sections, conclusion, references). Headings are clear and flow is generally coherent, fitting the criterion for âappropriate structure throughoutâ (band 2).

The cited documents (investor reports, CNBC interview, market-cap data sources) are directly relevant to market capitalisation and product revenues, and they supply quantitative figures and quotations. Depth is adequate but not wide-ranging: most insight is taken from headline figures rather than extensive internal data or contrasting viewpoints. This fits ârelevant and sufficiently in-depth,â band 3.
The IA includes all IB-required elements: title, research question, word count, contents page, references, and clearly-labelled supporting documents. Hence it earns full marks for presentation.
Four supporting documents are listed, satisfying the 3-5 requirement. Therefore band 3 is the correct placement for document count.

Both tools are applied to Nvidiaâs product and valuation data, and results are referenced in the discussion and conclusion. Nevertheless, analysis sometimes slips into narrative description (e.g., long explanations of P/E movement without linking back to strategic decisions), so relevance is present but not always crystal-clear. This aligns with band 3 (ârelevance not always clear but adequately appliedâ).

The student selects multiple, current data sets (market-cap series, P/E ratios, revenue segment figures) and displays them in graphs and a pie chart. Data are recent, specific and largely appropriate, though sources are all secondary. The breadth and appropriateness warrant âsufficient selection and use,â corresponding to band 4.

The analysis explains what the BCG positions imply for revenue contributions and links market-cap acceleration to the H100 launch, providing some interpretation of trendlines and ratios. Still, evaluation is surface-level, with limited consideration of alternate causes, time lags or external forces. The effort therefore illustrates âsome analysisâ but falls short of mostly effective, placing it in band 3.

The research questionâextent to which the H100 acted as a catalyst for increased market capâis directly answered in the concluding section, which attributes Nvidiaâs valuation surge largely to the H100. Hence the work meets the descriptor âexplicitly answers research question,â meriting band 3.

The IA employs one qualitative (BCG Matrix) and one quantitative (descriptive statistics on market-cap/P-E) tool. This meets adequacy but is not an especially broad or inventive toolkit for an HL study. Consequently, the work sits comfortably in the âadequate selectionâ band.

The conclusion restates findings, references revenue shares, market-cap growth and investor confidence, and these points do reflect the evidence cited earlier. No contradictory data are ignored, so consistency is strong enough for the top band.

The student maintains continuous focus on Nvidia, drawing revenue figures, market-cap data and product portfolio details directly from the firmâs reports and media sources. The BCG matrix, descriptive statistics and discussion of P/E ratio are all specifically tied back to Nvidiaâs strategy and outcome. While linkages are clear, some commentary is generic (e.g., âthis decision is intriguingâ) and the integration is occasionally interrupted by basic background narrative. The evidence points to strong analysis with partial integration, matching band 4.

The student explicitly adopts the key concept of change and repeatedly links it to Nvidiaâs shift from gaming GPUs to the H100 data-centre segment. Throughout the introduction, tool discussion and conclusion the word âchangeâ is woven into the narrative and used to frame the argument. However, the concept is treated largely at a descriptive level (e.g., âthis shows their changed focusâ) with only intermittent analytical depth about how the change affects strategic position; it is not fully threaded through every section of the analysis. Hence the work demonstrates a strong but only partially integrated understandingâbest aligned with band 4 rather than the consistently integrated approach demanded for band 5.

annotations.comment: Section numbering skips â3.â in the TOC; ensure headings and page references align to maintain coherence.
âNyidiaâ is a spelling error for Nvidia; maintain correct company name consistently throughout.
Background details on Tensor Cores and AI-optimized hardware strengthen context for H100âs importance.
annotations.groupName: Selection and Application of Tools and Theories

annotations.comment: The citation format in the footnote is inconsistent; standardize to one style (e.g., APA) for clarity.
Definition of Market Capitalization formula is accurate and aids quantitative analysis.
annotations.groupName: Selection and Application of Tools and Theories

annotations.comment: When discussing the bar graph, cite your data sources inline (e.g., share price data provider) to improve transparency.
Long sentence structure hampers readability; break into shorter sentences and correct comma placement.
Introduction to P/E ratio sets context well, preparing the reader for comparative analysis.
annotations.groupName: Selection and Application of Tools and Theories

annotations.comment: Typo in formula (âEarninasâ); use proper LaTeX notation: $$\text{P/E} = \frac{\text{Share Price}}{\text{Earnings per Share}}$$.
The comparative discussion with Intel adds evaluative depth by contrasting investor sentiment.
The title effectively references Nvidiaâs strategic change, though consider refining âStrategic change in the AI Eraâ for clarity and conciseness to better align with academic tone.
The research question is clear but split across two lines; rephrase into a single coherent sentence to improve readability and focus.
Explicit declaration of Key Concept â Change is a strong start, demonstrating clear integration of the central theme.
The table of contents is present and formatted, satisfying IB requirements; this enhances navigation.
Indentation of sub-items is inconsistent; apply uniform list formatting to improve presentation.
The introduction situates the H100 launch in context and explains its significance well.
The narrative is largely descriptive; deepen analysis by linking why this change strategically elevated Nvidiaâs position.
Integrate the key concept of change more analytically here by examining how each background feature contributed to strategic repositioning.
Selection of the BCG matrix is appropriate for product-portfolio analysis, demonstrating adequate tool choice.
annotations.groupName: Selection and Application of Tools and Theories

annotations.comment: The text describing Question Marks lacks depth; explain why cryptocurrency GPUs have high growth but low share and the strategic risk involved.
Phrasing âThe start the H100 seriesâ is unclear; revise for grammatical correctness and precision.
The concluding sentence clearly ties product revenues to H100âs impact on market cap.
The BCG matrix figure lacks axis labels or scales; include these to enhance interpretability.
Reposition the âBCG Matrixâ heading directly above the diagram for clearer linkage between text and figure.
Figure 4 and subsequent text reference âFigure 5â without labelling; ensure figure numbers and captions align.
The revenue-contribution text powerfully underscores data-centre dominance with quantitative figures.
Integrate percentages (e.g., 87.5%) directly into the narrative to quantify claims and support your analysis.
The pie chart effectively visualizes revenue breakdown, aiding comprehension of portfolio proportions.
The conclusion clearly restates findings and addresses the research question, demonstrating consistency.
The conclusion is comprehensive but lacks discussion of limitations or alternative factors; consider adding critique.
The final paragraph reiterates points already made; condense to improve conciseness and avoid redundancy.
Highlighting sustainability and long-term growth prospects in the conclusion adds depth to the rationale for strategic change.
Reference list includes varied sources, showing thorough research; ensure each entry follows the same citation style.
Citations use mixed superscript and inline formats; standardize to one consistent style throughout the document.
